introduction: This specification defines the HTTP handler layer for user administration and self-service profile management in apikit. It covers admin-only CRUD, role, and lifecycle endpoints under `/users`, self-service profile endpoints under `/user`, and admin access to per-user credential listings and revocation. Note: requirements referencing `apikit`, `auth`, and `db` package APIs are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against the installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.APIGroup: The Echo route group returned by `server.APIGroup()` on which all user management routes are registered.
glossary.APIKeyMeta: Go struct representing the non-secret metadata fields of an API key.
glossary.CreateUserRequest: Go struct representing the JSON request body for `POST /users`.
glossary.PATMeta: Go struct representing the non-secret metadata fields of a personal access token.
glossary.RegisterUserHandlers: The exported constructor function that registers all user management routes on the Echo API group.
glossary.UpdateUserRequest: Go struct representing the JSON request body for `PATCH /users/:id` and `PATCH /user`.
glossary.User: Go struct representing the JSON response object for user endpoints.
glossary.X-Request-ID: HTTP request header injected by the server core containing a unique identifier for the current request, read from Echo context.
glossary.active admin count: The count of users in the `users` table where `role = 'admin'` AND `status = 'active'`, used by the demote safeguard.
glossary.admin_bootstrap: The one-time startup mechanism (spec 04) that seeds the first admin user via an OAuth callback intercept; independent of `POST /users`.
glossary.api_keys table: The SQLite database table storing API key records, created and owned by the `database_layer` spec.
glossary.apikit.APIError: Helper function that formats and sends a standard JSON error response with a given HTTP status code and message.
glossary.apikit.CacheNoStore: Middleware or helper that sets `Cache-Control: no-store` on responses.
glossary.apikit.CheckETag: Helper function that compares the `If-None-Match` request header against the current resource ETag and returns true when they match (cache hit).
glossary.apikit.SetETag: Helper function that sets the `ETag` response header based on a resource timestamp.
glossary.auth.GetAuthInfo: Helper that retrieves the full authentication info struct from the Echo context.
glossary.auth.GetUserID: Helper that retrieves the authenticated user's UUID from the Echo context.
glossary.auth.RequireAdmin: Middleware helper that returns 403 if the calling user does not hold the admin role.
glossary.auth.RequirePermission: Middleware helper that checks whether the authenticated context (PAT or session) holds a specified permission, returning 403 if not.
glossary.db.ErrNotFound: Sentinel error returned by the database layer when a queried record does not exist.
glossary.db.FormatTime: Utility function that formats a `time.Time` value as an RFC 3339 UTC timestamp string.
glossary.include_blocked: Optional boolean query parameter on `GET /users` that controls whether blocked users are included in the response.
glossary.org_members table: The SQLite database table storing organization membership records, created and owned by the `database_layer` spec.
glossary.orgs table: The SQLite database table storing organization records, created and owned by the `database_layer` spec.
glossary.orgs:read: PAT permission scope required for listing a user's own organization memberships.
glossary.pats table: The SQLite database table storing personal access token records, created and owned by the `database_layer` spec.
glossary.provider: A free-form string stored on a user record identifying the OAuth provider (e.g. `"github"`, `"google"`); not validated against the provider registry during admin user creation.
glossary.provider_id: The identity token issued by the OAuth provider that uniquely identifies a user within that provider.
glossary.revoked_at: A nullable RFC 3339 timestamp column on `api_keys` and `pats` tables indicating when a credential was revoked; NULL means the credential is still active.
glossary.users table: The SQLite database table storing user records, created and owned by the `database_layer` spec.
glossary.users:read: PAT permission scope required for reading a user's own profile and updating own `full_name`.
glossary.\"active\": User status value indicating the user account is active and can authenticate.
glossary.\"admin\": User role value indicating the user has admin-level access to all endpoints and resources.
glossary.\"api key not found\": Error message returned with HTTP 404 when the specified API key does not exist for the given user.
glossary.\"blocked\": User status value indicating the user account is blocked and cannot authenticate.
glossary.\"cannot demote the last admin\": Error message returned with HTTP 409 when attempting to demote the only remaining admin user.
glossary.\"insufficient permissions\": Error message returned with HTTP 403 when a PAT lacks the required permission for the requested operation.
glossary.\"internal server error\": Error message returned with HTTP 500 when an unexpected database or server error occurs.
glossary.\"invalid request body\": Error message returned with HTTP 400 when the request body cannot be parsed as valid JSON.
glossary.\"missing required field: <field_name>\": Error message template returned with HTTP 400 when a required field is missing or empty in the request body.
glossary.\"missing required field: full_name\": Error message returned with HTTP 400 when the full_name field is missing from a PATCH request body.
glossary.\"provider identity already exists\": Error message returned with HTTP 409 when the (provider, provider_id) combination already exists in the users table.
glossary.\"token not found\": Error message returned with HTTP 404 when the specified PAT does not exist for the given user.
glossary.\"user not found\": Error message returned with HTTP 404 when the specified user ID does not exist in the users table.
glossary.\"user\": User role value indicating the user has regular (non-admin) access to their own resources only.
glossary.\"username already exists\": Error message returned with HTTP 409 when the username already exists in the users table.
glossary.(provider, provider_id): Composite unique constraint on the users table ensuring that each OAuth provider identity maps to exactly one user.
glossary.*sql.DB: Go standard library database handle type used for executing SQL queries against the SQLite database.
glossary.FullName *string: Pointer-to-string field in UpdateUserRequest, allowing distinction between an omitted field (nil) and an explicitly empty string.
glossary.If-None-Match: HTTP request header used for conditional GET requests; the server returns HTTP 304 when the header value matches the current ETag.
glossary.WHERE status = 'active': SQL filter clause that excludes blocked users from query results.
glossary.[]: Empty JSON array, returned when a list endpoint matches no results.
glossary.apikit.CheckETag(c, user.UpdatedAt): Helper function call that compares the If-None-Match request header against the resource ETag and returns true if the client cache is current.
glossary.apikit.SetETag(c, user.UpdatedAt): Helper function call that sets a weak ETag response header derived from the user resource updated_at timestamp.
glossary.auth.GetUserID(c): Helper function call that retrieves the authenticated user UUID from the Echo request context.
glossary.auth.RequireAdmin(c): Helper function call that returns HTTP 403 if the authenticated credential does not have admin-level access.
glossary.auth.RequirePermission(c, \"orgs\", \"re: Helper function call that returns HTTP 403 if the authenticated PAT does not have the orgs:read permission.
glossary.auth.RequirePermission(c, \"users\", \"r: Helper function call that returns HTTP 403 if the authenticated PAT does not have the users:read permission.
glossary.created_at: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp field recording when the resource was created.
glossary.email: User email address field, required at creation.
glossary.expires_at: Nullable RFC 3339 UTC timestamp field recording when a credential expires; null when the credential has no expiry.
glossary.full_name: Optional user display name field; the only field mutable via PATCH endpoints.
glossary.func(c echo.Context) error: Go function signature for Echo HTTP handler functions.
glossary.include_blocked=true: Query parameter value that includes blocked users in the GET /users list response.
glossary.key_id: Random 8-character alphanumeric identifier for an API key, used as the primary key in the api_keys table.
glossary.name: User-provided human-readable label for a PAT.
glossary.permissions: JSON array of resource_type:action strings defining the access scope of a PAT.
glossary.role: User field with value "admin" or "user" that determines access level.
glossary.role = \"admin\": Condition indicating the user has the admin role.
glossary.role = \"user\": Condition indicating the user has the regular (non-admin) role.
glossary.role = 'admin': SQL condition matching users with the admin role.
glossary.status: User field with value "active" or "blocked" that determines whether the user can authenticate.
glossary.status = \"active\": Condition indicating the user account is active.
glossary.status = \"blocked\": Condition indicating the user account is blocked.
glossary.status = 'active': SQL condition matching users with active status.
glossary.token_id: Random identifier for a PAT, used as the primary key in the pats table.
glossary.updated_at: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp field recording when the resource was last modified.
glossary.user.UpdatedAt: The updated_at timestamp of a User struct instance, used for ETag generation.
glossary.user_id: UUID field referencing the owning user in credential tables (api_keys, pats).
glossary.username: Unique user display name field, required at creation.
glossary.users:write: A hypothetical PAT permission that does not exist in the master PRD built-in permission set; self-service profile updates use users:read instead.

user_story.goal: register all user management routes on the Echo API group via a single constructor call
user_story.benefit: so that the server wires up user endpoints without needing per-handler registration logic scattered across the codebase

acceptance_criteria.action: registers all 15 routes (POST /users, GET /users, GET /users/:id, PATCH /users/:id, POST /users/:id/promote, POST /users/:id/demote, POST /users/:id/block, POST /users/:id/unblock, GET /users/:id/keys, DELETE /users/:id/keys/:key_id, GET /users/:id/tokens, DELETE /users/:id/tokens/:token_id, GET /user, PATCH /user, GET /user/orgs) on the provided Echo group and stores the `*sql.DB` handle for use by all handler functions
have the unexported signature `func(c echo.Context) error` and are registered exclusively through `RegisterUserHandlers`

user_story.goal: create a new user record via POST /users with username, email, provider, and provider_id
user_story.benefit: so that I can provision additional users without requiring them to complete an OAuth login flow

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, binds the `CreateUserRequest` body, generates a new UUID for the user ID, sets `role` to `"user"`, `status` to `"active"`, `full_name` to `""`, sets `created_at` and `updated_at` to the current UTC timestamp formatted via `db.FormatTime`, inserts the record into the `users table`, and returns the created `User` object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 with the complete `User` JSON object; all fields populated
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid POST /users request with all four required fields is received from an admin user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 400 error using `apikit.APIError` with message `"missing required field: <field_name>"` identifying the offending field
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400 with JSON error body containing the specific missing field name
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: any of the four required fields (`username`, `email`, `provider`, `provider_id`) is missing or is an empty string

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 400 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"invalid request body"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request body is not valid JSON

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 409 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"username already exists"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 409 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: insertion into the `users table` fails due to a unique constraint violation on `username`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 409 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"provider identity already exists"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 409 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: insertion into the `users table` fails due to a unique constraint violation on `(provider, provider_id)`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)` before any other processing
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

edge_cases.action: returns a 500 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"internal server error"` without leaking database internals
edge_cases.error_condition: the database INSERT call returns an unexpected (non-constraint) error

edge_cases.action: stores the `provider` field as provided by the admin without any validation against the OAuth provider registry, accepting any non-empty string

user_story.goal: retrieve a list of all users, optionally including blocked users
user_story.benefit: so that I can audit the user population and manage user lifecycle

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, queries the `users table` filtering `WHERE status = 'active'`, and returns the result array
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with a JSON array of `User` objects excluding blocked users; empty array `[]` if none match
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET /users request is received from an admin user without the `include_blocked` parameter

acceptance_criteria.action: queries the `users table` without a status filter, returning all users regardless of status
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with a JSON array of all `User` objects including blocked users
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET /users request is received with query parameter `include_blocked=true`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

edge_cases.action: returns an empty JSON array rather than null
edge_cases.trigger: the `users table` contains no rows matching the filter

edge_cases.action: returns a 500 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"internal server error"`
edge_cases.error_condition: the database query returns an unexpected error

user_story.goal: retrieve a single user record by UUID
user_story.benefit: so that I can inspect a specific user's current state

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, queries the `users table` by ID, sets the ETag via `apikit.SetETag(c, user.UpdatedAt)`, checks `If-None-Match` via `apikit.CheckETag(c, user.UpdatedAt)`, and returns the user object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the `User` JSON object and ETag header set; HTTP 304 if the client's ETag matches
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET /users/:id request is received from an admin user for an existing user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no user with the given ID exists in the `users table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

edge_cases.action: returns 304 without a response body after `apikit.CheckETag` confirms the cache is current
edge_cases.trigger: the client sends an `If-None-Match` header whose value matches the current ETag derived from `user.UpdatedAt`

user_story.goal: update a user's full_name via PATCH /users/:id
user_story.benefit: so that I can correct display names without recreating the user record

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, binds the `UpdateUserRequest` body, fetches the user from the `users table`, updates `full_name` and `updated_at`, persists the change, and returns the updated user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the updated `User` JSON object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid PATCH /users/:id request with a `full_name` field is received from an admin user for an existing user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 400 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"missing required field: full_name"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request body does not include the `full_name` field (field is absent, not just empty string)

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no user with the given ID exists in the `users table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

edge_cases.action: accepts the value and clears the user's `full_name` to an empty string, persisting and returning the updated user
edge_cases.trigger: the `full_name` field in the request body is an empty string

edge_cases.action: uses `UpdateUserRequest` with `FullName *string` (pointer) to distinguish a missing field from an empty-string value; a nil pointer means the field was absent and triggers a 400 error

user_story.goal: promote a user to the admin role via POST /users/:id/promote
user_story.benefit: so that I can delegate administrative capabilities to trusted users

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, fetches the user from the `users table`, updates `role` to `"admin"` and `updated_at`, persists the change, and returns the updated user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the updated `User` JSON object with `role` set to `"admin"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST /users/:id/promote request is received from an admin user for an existing non-admin user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the current user object unchanged without modifying the database (idempotent)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the existing `User` JSON object unmodified

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no user with the given ID exists in the `users table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

user_story.goal: demote a user from the admin role via POST /users/:id/demote while preventing removal of the last admin
user_story.benefit: so that I can revoke administrative access without risking total lockout

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, fetches the user from the `users table`, counts users with `role = 'admin'` AND `status = 'active'`, updates `role` to `"user"` and `updated_at`, persists the change, and returns the updated user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the updated `User` JSON object with `role` set to `"user"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST /users/:id/demote request is received from an admin user for an existing admin user when more than one active admin exists

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the current user object unchanged without modifying the database (idempotent)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the existing `User` JSON object unmodified

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 409 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"cannot demote the last admin"` without modifying the user record
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 409 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the target user is the only remaining user with `role = 'admin'` AND `status = 'active'` (the `active admin count` equals 1)

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no user with the given ID exists in the `users table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

edge_cases.action: returns a 500 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"internal server error"` without modifying the user record
edge_cases.error_condition: the `active admin count` query returns a database error

user_story.goal: block a user account via POST /users/:id/block
user_story.benefit: so that I can revoke authentication access for compromised or inactive accounts without deleting audit trail data

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, fetches the user from the `users table`, updates `status` to `"blocked"` and `updated_at`, persists the change, and returns the updated user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the updated `User` JSON object with `status` set to `"blocked"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST /users/:id/block request is received from an admin user for an existing active user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the current user object unchanged without modifying the database (idempotent)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the existing `User` JSON object unmodified

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no user with the given ID exists in the `users table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

user_story.goal: unblock a previously blocked user via POST /users/:id/unblock
user_story.benefit: so that I can restore authentication access after a block was applied in error or the situation has been resolved

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, fetches the user from the `users table`, updates `status` to `"active"` and `updated_at`, persists the change, and returns the updated user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the updated `User` JSON object with `status` set to `"active"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST /users/:id/unblock request is received from an admin user for an existing blocked user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the current user object unchanged without modifying the database (idempotent)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the existing `User` JSON object unmodified

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no user with the given ID exists in the `users table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

user_story.goal: list metadata for all API keys belonging to a specific user
user_story.benefit: so that I can audit credential usage without exposing plaintext secrets

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, verifies the user exists in the `users table`, queries the `api_keys table` for all keys with matching `user_id`, and returns the metadata array
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with a JSON array of `APIKeyMeta` objects; empty array `[]` if no keys exist; `revoked_at` and `expires_at` are null-able RFC 3339 strings
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET /users/:id/keys request is received from an admin user for an existing user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"` without querying the `api_keys table`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no user with the given ID exists in the `users table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

edge_cases.action: never includes plaintext secret values in any field of the returned `APIKeyMeta` objects; only `key_id`, `user_id`, `created_at`, `expires_at`, and `revoked_at` are returned

user_story.goal: revoke a specific API key belonging to a user via DELETE /users/:id/keys/:key_id
user_story.benefit: so that I can immediately invalidate compromised or unused credentials

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, queries the `api_keys table` for the key matching both `key_id` and `user_id`, sets `revoked_at` to the current UTC timestamp, and returns no body
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 204 No Content
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a DELETE /users/:id/keys/:key_id request is received from an admin user for an existing, active API key

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 204 without modifying the record (idempotent)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 204 No Content with no response body
acceptance_criteria.state: the key exists in the `api_keys table` but `revoked_at` is not NULL

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"api key not found"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no key matching the given `key_id` and `user_id` combination exists in the `api_keys table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

user_story.goal: list metadata for all personal access tokens belonging to a specific user
user_story.benefit: so that I can audit token usage and permissions without exposing plaintext secrets

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, verifies the user exists in the `users table`, queries the `pats table` for all tokens with matching `user_id`, and returns the metadata array
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with a JSON array of `PATMeta` objects; empty array `[]` if no tokens exist; `revoked_at` and `expires_at` are nullable RFC 3339 strings
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET /users/:id/tokens request is received from an admin user for an existing user

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"` without querying the `pats table`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no user with the given ID exists in the `users table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

edge_cases.action: never includes plaintext token secrets in any field of the returned `PATMeta` objects; only `token_id`, `name`, `permissions`, `user_id`, `created_at`, `expires_at`, and `revoked_at` are returned

user_story.goal: revoke a specific personal access token belonging to a user via DELETE /users/:id/tokens/:token_id
user_story.benefit: so that I can immediately invalidate compromised or unused tokens across the user base

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, queries the `pats table` for the token matching both `token_id` and `user_id`, sets `revoked_at` to the current UTC timestamp, and returns no body
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 204 No Content
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a DELETE /users/:id/tokens/:token_id request is received from an admin user for an existing, active PAT

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 204 without modifying the record (idempotent)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 204 No Content with no response body
acceptance_criteria.state: the token exists in the `pats table` but `revoked_at` is not NULL

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 404 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"token not found"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no token matching the given `token_id` and `user_id` combination exists in the `pats table`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body containing `"forbidden"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the caller does not hold admin-level access

user_story.goal: retrieve my own profile via GET /user without needing to know my user ID
user_story.benefit: so that I can display and inspect my current account state in client applications

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "users", "read")`, retrieves the user ID via `auth.GetUserID(c)`, queries the `users table`, sets the ETag via `apikit.SetETag(c, user.UpdatedAt)`, checks `If-None-Match` via `apikit.CheckETag(c, user.UpdatedAt)`, and returns the user object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the `User` JSON object and ETag header set; HTTP 304 if the client's ETag matches
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET /user request is received from an authenticated user with the `users:read` permission

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequirePermission` with message `"insufficient permissions"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the PAT or session context lacks the `users:read` permission

edge_cases.trigger: the client sends an `If-None-Match` header whose value matches the current ETag derived from `user.UpdatedAt`

user_story.goal: update my own full_name via PATCH /user
user_story.benefit: so that I can correct my display name without administrative intervention

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "users", "read")`, retrieves the user ID via `auth.GetUserID(c)`, binds the `UpdateUserRequest` body, fetches the user from the `users table`, updates `full_name` and `updated_at`, persists the change, and returns the updated user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the updated `User` JSON object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid PATCH /user request with a `full_name` field is received from an authenticated user with the `users:read` permission

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a 400 error via `apikit.APIError` with message `"missing required field: full_name"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 400 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request body does not include the `full_name` field

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequirePermission` with message `"insufficient permissions"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the PAT or session context lacks the `users:read` permission

edge_cases.action: uses the `users:read` permission check (not a write permission) because the master PRD PAT permission set does not include a `users:write` scope; self-service `full_name` updates are scoped within `users:read`

user_story.goal: list my organization memberships via GET /user/orgs
user_story.benefit: so that I can discover which organizations I belong to and their current status

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "orgs", "read")`, retrieves the user ID via `auth.GetUserID(c)`, queries the `org_members table` joined with the `orgs table` filtering for active organizations where the user is a member, and returns the organization array
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with a JSON array of organization objects containing `id`, `name`, `slug`, `url`, `status`, `created_at`, `updated_at`; empty array `[]` if no active memberships exist
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET /user/orgs request is received from an authenticated user with the `orgs:read` permission

acceptance_criteria.action: returns 403 via `auth.RequirePermission` with message `"insufficient permissions"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with JSON error body
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the PAT or session context lacks the `orgs:read` permission

edge_cases.action: excludes the blocked organization from the returned array; only active organizations appear in the response
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 200 with JSON array omitting blocked organizations
edge_cases.state: an organization the user is a member of has status `"blocked"`

edge_cases.action: returns an empty JSON array rather than null
edge_cases.state: the user has no organization memberships or all memberships are in blocked organizations

correctness_properties.title: Last admin safeguard is unbreakable
correctness_properties.for_any: sequence of promote and demote operations
correctness_properties.invariant: at least one user with `role = 'admin'` AND `status = 'active'` always exists in the `users table` after any demote operation completes; the system refuses to perform a demote that would reduce the `active admin count` to zero

correctness_properties.title: Action endpoints are idempotent
correctness_properties.for_any: repeated calls to promote, demote, block, or unblock with the same user ID
correctness_properties.invariant: the response is always HTTP 200 with the current user state, and the `users table` record is unchanged after the second and subsequent calls when the user is already in the target state

correctness_properties.title: Credential revocation is idempotent and always returns 204
correctness_properties.for_any: repeated DELETE calls to revoke the same API key or PAT
correctness_properties.invariant: every successful call (key or token found) returns HTTP 204 No Content regardless of whether `revoked_at` was already set; the `revoked_at` timestamp is never overwritten once set

correctness_properties.title: Credential listings never expose secrets
correctness_properties.invariant: no response field contains a plaintext API key secret or PAT secret; returned objects contain only the metadata fields defined in `APIKeyMeta` and `PATMeta`

correctness_properties.title: Admin-only endpoints enforce auth before any data access
correctness_properties.invariant: `auth.RequireAdmin(c)` is the first call in every handler; no database query against the `users table`, `api_keys table`, or `pats table` is executed if the caller is not an admin

correctness_properties.title: List endpoints return empty array, never null
correctness_properties.for_any: GET /users, GET /users/:id/keys, GET /users/:id/tokens, GET /user/orgs when no records match
correctness_properties.invariant: the response body is the JSON literal `[]` and the HTTP status is 200, not a null value or a 404 error

steps.action: sends POST /users with JSON body containing username, email, provider, and provider_id, authenticated via admin session or API key
routes the request to the createUser handler registered by `RegisterUserHandlers`
calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)` to verify admin access; returns 403 if not admin
binds the request body into `CreateUserRequest` and validates all four fields are non-empty; returns 400 if any are missing
generates a new UUID, sets defaults (role=user, status=active, full_name empty), and inserts a new record into the `users table` via `*sql.DB`
detects unique constraint violations and returns 409 if username or (provider, provider_id) already exists
serializes the new `User` struct and returns HTTP 201 to the admin client

execution_paths.title: Admin demotes a user with last-admin safeguard

steps.action: sends POST /users/:id/demote for a target admin user
calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)` to verify caller is admin
fetches the target user from the `users table`; returns 404 if not found
checks if target already has role=user and returns 200 unchanged if so (idempotent)
queries the `users table` to count the `active admin count`
returns HTTP 409 with message 'cannot demote the last admin' if the count equals 1
updates the target user's role to 'user' and updated_at in the `users table`, then returns HTTP 200 with the updated `User` object

execution_paths.title: Admin revokes a user's API key (idempotent)

steps.action: sends DELETE /users/:id/keys/:key_id for a specific user and key
calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)` to verify admin access
queries the `api_keys table` for the row matching both key_id and user_id; returns 404 if not found
checks if `revoked_at` is already non-NULL; if so, returns HTTP 204 immediately without a database write
sets `revoked_at` to the current UTC timestamp in the `api_keys table`
returns HTTP 204 No Content to the admin client

execution_paths.title: Authenticated user reads and updates own profile

steps.action: sends GET /user with a PAT bearing the `users:read` permission
calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "users", "read")`; returns 403 if permission is absent
calls `auth.GetUserID(c)` to retrieve the authenticated user's UUID from context
queries the `users table` for the user record and sets the ETag via `apikit.SetETag`; returns 304 if `If-None-Match` matches
returns HTTP 200 with the `User` JSON object
sends PATCH /user with JSON body containing full_name and the same PAT
calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "users", "read")`, retrieves user ID, updates `full_name` and `updated_at` in the `users table`, and returns HTTP 200 with the updated `User` object

execution_paths.title: Full end-to-end: admin provisions user, promotes, then user views own orgs

steps.action: sends POST /users with valid body to create a new user; receives HTTP 201 with the new `User` object including the generated UUID
routes the request through the Echo group registered by `RegisterUserHandlers` against the `*sql.DB` handle provided by database_layer
calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)` (auth_middleware), inserts into the `users table` (database_layer), and returns HTTP 201
sends POST /users/:id/promote for the newly created user's UUID
calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)`, fetches the user from `users table`, updates role to admin, returns HTTP 200 with updated `User`
sends GET /user/orgs authenticated with a PAT bearing the `orgs:read` permission

calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, "orgs", "read")` (auth_middleware), calls `auth.GetUserID(c)`, queries `org_members table` joined with `orgs table` (database_layer) excluding blocked orgs, and returns HTTP 200 with the organization array

error_handling.condition: caller is not an admin on any /users endpoint
error_handling.behavior: handler calls `auth.RequireAdmin(c)` which responds with HTTP 403 and message `"forbidden"` via `apikit.APIError`; no further processing occurs

error_handling.condition: PAT lacks required permission on /user or /user/orgs endpoint
error_handling.behavior: handler calls `auth.RequirePermission(c, ...)` which responds with HTTP 403 and message `"insufficient permissions"` via `apikit.APIError`; no further processing occurs

error_handling.condition: request body is missing or has an empty required field (username, email, provider, provider_id, or full_name)
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 400 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"missing required field: <field_name>"` identifying the specific offending field

error_handling.condition: request body is not valid JSON
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 400 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"invalid request body"`

error_handling.condition: queried user ID does not exist in the `users table` (database returns `db.ErrNotFound`)
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 404 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"user not found"`

error_handling.condition: queried API key matching (key_id, user_id) does not exist in the `api_keys table`
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 404 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"api key not found"`

error_handling.condition: queried PAT matching (token_id, user_id) does not exist in the `pats table`
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 404 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"token not found"`

error_handling.condition: INSERT into `users table` fails due to unique constraint on `username`
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 409 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"username already exists"`

error_handling.condition: INSERT into `users table` fails due to unique constraint on (provider, provider_id)
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 409 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"provider identity already exists"`

error_handling.condition: demote attempted when `active admin count` equals 1
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 409 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"cannot demote the last admin"` without modifying the `users table`

error_handling.condition: any database operation returns an unexpected error not matching `db.ErrNotFound` or a constraint violation
error_handling.behavior: handler returns HTTP 500 via `apikit.APIError` with message `"internal server error"` without leaking internal error details

symbols.name: Group
symbols.notes: Echo route group; all user management routes are registered on the instance returned by server.APIGroup()

symbols.name: Context
symbols.notes: Echo request context passed to every handler function

symbols.name: New
symbols.notes: Generates a new random UUID v4 for user IDs at creation time
